content:
References take from the story “A Worn Path” by Eudora Welty

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 9, 2025 4:28PM
Karima Laurimore
Hi Michelle,

I enjoyed reading your post in regard to the main character, Pheonix Jackson. I agree with you stated in that Pheonix shows great determination and strength to complete her mission to get the medication that her grandson needs. I believe that though her age, fragile body and mental decline she continued to show resilience and Pheonix was very smart and aware of her surroundings. I enjoyed the comparison you made of the name, "Pheonix" versus the mythical bird I agree with you on the symbolism between the two.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 9, 2025 4:42PM
Karima Laurimore
Hi Conganige,

I enjoyed reading your post. I enjoyed the way you included that the story reflected a broader societal and personal hardships. Pheonix Jackson was definitely a woman of strength, will and determination. She didn't let the obstacles of "the path" stand in her way. In the story she comes across the white man who I believe had just finished hunting he tries to intimidate her into going back home. I believe this also had a societal issue of racism for that time and the hardship of already being African American woman, ageing, and losing her memory at times does symbolize the great strength that she carries.
